blocks.content: DSP supports OAuth 2.0 authentication using Salesforce security features, including:
Complete the steps below to configure OAuth 2.0 authentication between the Connect Org and the DSP App Org.
1. Create an External Client App in the Connect Org

children.content: Create an External Client App to generate the Consumer Key and Consumer Secret required for OAuth authentication.

blocks.content: 2. Create an Auth. Provider in the DSP App Org

children.content: Create an Auth. Provider to connect the DSP App Org to the External Client App created in the Connect Org.
Consumer Key, Consumer Secret: Copy from the External Client App in the Connect Org.
Authorize Endpoint URL: Use "https://test.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize" for sandbox environment or "https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize" for production.
Token Endpoint URL: Use "https://test.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token" for sandbox environment or "https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token" for production.
Default Scopes: Enter required scopes. Separate multiple scopes with spaces.

blocks.content: 3. Update Callback URL in the Connect Org

children.content: Update the External Client App to use the Callback URL generated in the DSP App Org.

blocks.content: 4. Create a Named Credential in the DSP App Org

children.content: URL: Enter the Connect Org domain URL (found in My Domain Settings).
Auth Provider: Select the Auth Provider created earlier.
Scope: Enter the OAuth scopes enabled in the Connect Org's External Client App. Separate multiple scopes with spaces.

blocks.content: 5. Create a DSP Connection in the DSP App Org
